message: Jake
Cindy
Tim
Hey,
that's
the bartender.
Well, he looked unwell, so I sent him home.
Is that so?
The bartender.
Yeah.
If you want, why not go visit him?
Ah, Detective.
I had something I wanted to talk about.
I also have a few things I'd like to ask.
I’m glad to see you.
Detective.
Look.
Talk.
Listen.
Think.
Move.
System.
Around here.
About Ariana.
About Gloria.
About things.
About　things.
About Gloria.
To another place.
Save.
Exit the game.
Once again, no customers can be seen. The lighting is dimmed, and the music has stopped, leaving the inside of the bar devoid of life, just like right after closing.
Looking at the floor and the counter, which don't have a single stain, it seems that not a single customer has visited the shop today.
The bored waiter is watching me and
us with great interest. If he’s that free, he should just close the shop.
Unlike his usual calm expression for his age, he looks a bit fidgety and anxious.
Can't say he’s not very impressive,
that's something I shouldn't say.
He likely has
hidden virtues that only I can see.
If he just stopped dressing so shabby and dyed his hair pink, he could reintegrate into society right away.
I really think he should do that.
Actually, Ariana hasn't come back home yet. Has she gone back to work?
No, that’s not the case.
Actually, she even skipped the mother-daughter meeting.
What, really? She was so looking forward to that.
I’m really wondering where she went.
I don't know what to do. I'm extremely anxious.
Please,
tell her to do her best if you see Ariana.
You know, Ariana has been missing since the evening before the mother-daughter meeting. I thought she just went straight to the Guggenheim.
No, she seems to not have gone.
No way, she wouldn’t break a promise.
Could it be that Gloria took her?
Gloria?
Yeah. I mean, it must be tough for her at work without Ariana, right? So, they might have forced her to go.
I wonder if she’s okay.
To the apartment.
Could you go to her apartment and check?
Sorry, I don’t know where it is.
I know. As long as he’s safe, that’s what matters. He can go back to work if he wants. But I’m worried.
Yeah, right. Where is it?
It's the Flora House apartment in the Upper East Side.
Detective, please take care of Ariana.
By any chance, have you heard from Gloria?
No, not at all. But that woman might have figured out where we are.
That is certainly possible, but...
As long as Ariana’s safe, that’s all that matters.
I hope that woman hasn’t done anything terrible to her.
But it’s not like it’s been decided that Gloria took her.
So then what the hell does that mean? There's no other explanation.
That woman.
If she did something to Ariana...
I won’t forgive her.
It seems like he’s losing his composure more than when he failed the audition. What on earth happened?
He clearly doesn’t have good feelings towards Gloria.
I get that you’re worried about...
If Gloria...
abducted her, she shouldn't be in any danger. There could be something worse.
Why does he hate Gloria so much?
Is it because he was criticized for dating her? I should ask him when I get the chance.
Where in the world is she?
She vanished the day before the mother-daughter meeting? So where could she be?
Maybe she was taken back by Gloria.
I hope it’s nothing bad.
It’s quite possible that Gloria...
abducted her.
But I can’t believe she would do something like that.
No, she would, right? Yeah, that seems plausible.
If I hear anything from Gloria, I’ll understand.
Let me know if you find out anything.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
